title: alnus alnobetula f. mollis
intro: Alnus alnobetula f. mollis, also known as the Mountain Alder or White Alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Betulaceae, characterized by its greyish-brown bark and slender branches. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Alnus alnobetula f. mollis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Herbal preparations of Alnus alnobetula f. mollis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and other ailments.
